steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage claims discoveries "have persisted into the 2000s." The answer must cite a hoard discovered in the 2000s to support that claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the Broighter Hoard, Coggalbeg Hoard, and Carrick-on-Suir Hoard have all been found since 1896.: mixes a 2000s find with pre-2000 data, diluting the evidence for the specific claim. ✗
  • Choice B — the Carrick-on-Suir Hoard was found in 2013.: the only hoard in the table discovered in the 2000s — directly supports the passage's claim. ✓
  • Choice C — the Carrick-on-Suir Hoard was found after the Broighter Hoard.: is true but does not highlight the 2000s timeframe required by the claim. ✗
  • Choice D — the Broighter Hoard and Coggalbeg Hoard were both found after 2000.: is factually wrong because Broighter was discovered in 1896 and Coggalbeg in 1945, not after 2000. ✗
That matches Choice B — the Carrick-on-Suir Hoard was found in 2013..
